skills.prompt: This topic covers Interpret picture graphs for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.10(A)). Students read picture graphs to answer questions. Key concepts include using the key, counting/multiplying symbols, comparing categories, and computing totals and differences. Vocabulary: picture graph, key, symbol, data, most, least, more, fewer, total, difference. Common misconceptions include ignoring the key for scaled graphs and confusing 'how many more' with 'how many.' Activities should include reading pre-made graphs, answering multi-step questions, and creating peer questions appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content for this topic, use data sets that are meaningful to 7-8-year-olds, such as favorite foods, weather observations, or classroom surveys.

This topic covers Interpret bar graphs I for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.10(A)). Students read bar graphs to answer questions. Key concepts include reading the scale, comparing bar heights, computing differences and totals, and reading both horizontal and vertical formats. Vocabulary: bar graph, scale, axis, category, value, compare, total, difference. Common misconceptions include misreading non-1 scales and confusing axes. Activities should include answering questions, comparing data, and multi-step problem solving appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content for this topic, use data sets that are meaningful to 7-8-year-olds, such as favorite foods, weather observations, or classroom surveys. Encourage students to formulate questions that data can answer, collect and organize information systematically, and draw evidence-based conclusions.

This topic covers Create picture graphs for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.10(B)). Students create or verify picture graphs where symbols represent data. Key concepts include choosing symbols, using keys (especially for scaled graphs where each symbol represents >1), organizing data in rows/columns, and ensuring accuracy. Vocabulary: picture graph, pictograph, symbol, key, data, category, represent, title, label, scale. Common misconceptions include forgetting the key and miscounting. Activities should include collecting data, creating graphs, and evaluating correctness appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content for this topic, use data sets that are meaningful to 7-8-year-olds, such as favorite foods, weather observations, or classroom surveys.

This topic covers Create bar graphs for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.10(B)). Students create or verify bar graphs. Key concepts include drawing and labeling axes, choosing scales, drawing bars to correct heights, and adding titles. Vocabulary: bar graph, axis, scale, bar, height, label, title, category, horizontal, vertical. Common misconceptions include not starting at zero and inconsistent bar widths. Activities should include data collection, graph creation, and accuracy checking appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content for this topic, use data sets that are meaningful to 7-8-year-olds, such as favorite foods, weather observations, or classroom surveys. Encourage students to formulate questions that data can answer, collect and organize information systematically, and draw evidence-based conclusions.
